name: Dar-us-Sakina
mission: DAR-US-SAKINA IS A 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO INCLUSION OF INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES IN THE MUSLIM COMMUNITY OF HOUSTON. OUR PLATFORM IS A SAFE SPACE FOR PEOPLE OF ALL ABILITIES AND THEIR CAREGIVERS. WE INSPIRE THE COMMUNITY TO ABANDON STIGMAS AND EMBRACE INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES WITH EMPATHY AND RESPECT. TOGETHER, WE ARE BETTER.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2021 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: Support for Community Awareness Programs (Islamic Relief USA)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dar-us-Sakina explicitly states: "A large proportion of our programming is Zakat-eligible, allowing your donation to directly uplift individuals and families facing ongoing financial hardship due to disability." They also mention reserving "its Zakat fund for Muslim families who are in financial need or crisis." Furthermore, they have a "Zakat Program" and "Zakat Fund" to provide comprehensive support for special needs families in crisis, including emergency respite care, counseling, and durable medical equipment. On donation platforms, 'Zakat' is listed as a campaign option. (Source: https://www.every.org/dar-us-sakina)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)

They also mention reserving "its Zakat fund for Muslim families who are in financial need or crisis." all_citations.claim: Dar-us-Sakina holds a Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100 and a program expense ratio of 72.2%.
The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a zakat policy.
The charity reported total revenue of $1,115,693 and holds 15.9 months of working capital.
The mission is to provide inclusive opportunities for individuals with disabilities in the Houston Muslim community.
The organization focuses on empowering individuals with disabilities.
The charity serves the specific cultural and religious needs of the Houston community.
Dar-Us-Sakina operates programs that provide educational and social outcomes for participants.
The organization supports families through a Zakat Fund and tracks inclusive educational support hours.
Volunteer enrichment programs are a core part of the organization's engagement strategy.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its strong financial health and high alignment with religious donor values, though its impact score is tempered by a focus on capacity building rather than direct cost-efficiency metrics.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance impact reporting to provide more granular cost-per-beneficiary data
Increase transparency regarding long-term outcome measurements

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is high for Muslim donors as the charity is zakat-eligible and addresses the urgent religious and cultural need for disability inclusion within the faith community [2][7]. The organization reported total revenue of $1,115,693, reflecting a stable track record of community support [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a commitment to capacity building through its Sakina Sunday School and internship programs [8][11]. While it maintains a solid program expense ratio of 72.2% and a Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100, the lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data results in a medium impact rating [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Dar-us-Sakina empowers individuals with disabilities in the Houston Muslim community through inclusive education and specialized support services.
strengths: High financial efficiency with a fundraising cost of only $0.06 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 15.9 months of working capital
Verified zakat-eligibility for religious donor alignment
baseline_narrative.summary: Dar-us-Sakina provides inclusive opportunities for individuals with disabilities in Houston, focusing on empowerment and community integration [4][5]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill religious obligations while supporting families through a dedicated Zakat Fund [2][9].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support specialized disability services within a Muslim communal context and value high financial accountability.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations for local families in need
Care about disability inclusion and special education
Value organizations with strong financial reserves and low overhead
Prefer supporting niche, underserved community needs
giving_considerations: The organization is local to Houston but serves as a model for others
Financial reserves are high, suggesting long-term stability rather than immediate cash need
Impact is measured through student progress rather than large-scale RCTs
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international humanitarian relief or those who require rigorous academic impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that specialized educational interventions are effectively meeting the unique needs of students.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.06 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of funds are available for direct services.
Dar-us-Sakina holds <cite id="1">15.9 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant safety net for long-term program sustainability.
The charity has <cite id="8">formal fatwa documentation</cite> for its programs and serves <cite id="2">verified zakat-eligible categories</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Dar-us-Sakina's operational strength is rooted in its <cite id="1">72.2% independent board members</cite>, which ensures objective governance and oversight. This governance structure supports a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 score in Culture & Community</cite> from Charity Navigator. Furthermore, the organization has successfully diversified its income, generating <cite id="3">$142,034 from program service revenue</cite> alongside <cite id="3">$398,320 in public contributions</cite>, indicating a sustainable mix of community support and service-based funding.
rich_narrative.summary: Dar-us-Sakina is a specialized nonprofit organization dedicated to <cite id="4">empowering individuals with disabilities</cite> within the Houston Muslim community. Operating with a mission to provide <cite id="5">inclusive opportunities and specialized support services</cite>, the organization addresses a critical gap in community infrastructure by offering programs such as the <cite id="8">Sakina Sunday School and the Adult Day Program (ADP)</cite>. Financially, the organization has demonstrated significant growth, reporting <cite id="3">$540,354 in total revenue for 2023</cite>, which represents a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.6%</cite>. The charity maintains a <cite id="1">93/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability and leadership. A cornerstone of their operations is the <cite id="9">dedicated Zakat Fund</cite>, which ensures that <cite id="2">zakat-eligible donations</cite> are directed toward <cite id="2">families in the fuqara and masakin categories</cite>. By integrating religious compliance with professional disability services, Dar-us-Sakina serves as a vital resource for <cite id="9">families seeking inclusive educational support</cite> and community belonging.
